prompt: A surreal, minimalist architectural diptych. Left panel: A close-up of a marble staircase ascending sharply into bright light. Right panel: A wide-angle view of dozens of identical small staircases floating in a void; while each individual staircase clearly angles upward, they are stepped downward in a massive formation, creating a global path that leads into a dark abyss. No text, no numbers, no people. Impossible Euclidean geometry, Escher-style lighting, high contrast, uncanny atmosphere, 8k resolution. --ar 16:9
visual_logic: Individual staircases slope upward (subgroup trend), but their collective arrangement creates a continuous downward trajectory (aggregate reversal). The diptych structure contrasts local perception against global reality.
insight: In 1973, Berkeley was accused of bias as men had higher aggregate admission rates, but data showed women actually had higher rates within most individual departments.
